ogTitle: Cleve vs Clifton Hills Station: Climate Safety

heading: Cleve vs Clifton Hills Station — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cleve, South Australia and Clifton Hills Station, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clifton Hills Station has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Cleve (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cleve or Clifton Hills Station safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cleve has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Clifton Hills Station scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cleve scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Clifton Hills Station scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cleve or Clifton Hills Station?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clifton Hills Station has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
